questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The term ‘accounting period’ is used to refer to the

options.A: time span during which taxes are paid to the Inland Revenue Board
options.B: Budget period, usually one year, relied on by the accountant
options.C: time span, usually one year covered by financial statement
options.D: period within which debtors are expected to settle accounts.

questions.ai_explanation: The accounting period refers to the time span, usually one year, covered by financial statements during which financial performance is measured and reported.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Which of the following factors’ has aided the develop- ment of Accounting?

options.B: The discovery of mineral resources in commercial quantity.
options.C: The growth in size of businesses and the separation of ownership and management.
options.D: The development and management of a sophisticated monetary system.

questions.ai_explanation: The growth in size of businesses and the separation of ownership from management necessitated the development of formal accounting systems to track and report financial activities.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Assigning revenues to the accounting period in which goods were sold or services rendered and expenses in- curred is known as
questions.ai_explanation: The matching concept requires that revenues be matched with the expenses incurred in earning them within the same accounting period.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Which of the following concepts stipulates that account- ing profit is the difference between revenue and expenses?
questions.ai_explanation: The accrual concept stipulates that accounting profit is the difference between revenue earned and expenses incurred during a period, regardless of cash flows.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The accounting convention which states that ‘profit must not be recognized until realized while all losses should be adequately provided for’ is termed
questions.ai_explanation: The conservatism (or prudence) convention states that profits should not be recognized until realized, while all potential losses should be adequately provided for.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: An effective accounting system should provide infor- mation

options.C: on internal and external reporting for managers and third parties
options.D: for promoters, directors, labour unions and distributors.

questions.ai_explanation: An effective accounting system should provide information for both internal reporting (for managers) and external reporting (for third parties like investors and creditors).

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Accounting information is used by investors and credi- tors of a company to predict

options.A: future cash flows of the company
options.B: future tax payments of the company
options.C: potential merger candidates for the company
options.D: appropriate remuneration for the company’s staff.

questions.ai_explanation: Investors and creditors use accounting information to assess and predict the company's future cash flows, which determine its ability to generate returns and repay obligations.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: When a business incurs labours cost in installing a fixed asset, the cost is treated as
questions.ai_explanation: Labour costs incurred in installing a fixed asset are capitalized as an additional cost of the asset since they are necessary to make the asset ready for use.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the cardinal rule of the double entry system?

options.A: Debit the increasing account and credit the decreasing account.
options.B: Debit the receiving account and credit the giving account.
options.C: Debit the asset account and credit the liability account.
options.D: Debit the revenue account and credit the expenditure account.

questions.ai_explanation: The cardinal rule of double entry bookkeeping is to debit the receiving account and credit the giving account for every transaction.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Malam Gambo bought a freezer for his shop costing N 10,500. In recording, he debited office expenses account and credited the bank account. What book keeping error has he committed?
questions.ai_explanation: This is an error of principle because a fixed asset (freezer) was incorrectly recorded as an expense (office expenses) instead of being capitalized as an asset.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The use of the folio in the ledger is for
questions.ai_explanation: The folio column in the ledger is used for referencing purposes, allowing cross-referencing between the journal and ledger entries.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Account of Tanko Nig Ltd. Dr. Cr. N N 1/10 Bal. b/f 5,000 2/10 Cash 3,000 Sales 10,000 15/10 Bank 6,500 What is the closing balance of the account shown above?
questions.ai_explanation: Opening balance N5,000 (Dr) + Cash N3,000 (Dr) + Bank N6,500 (Cr) + Sales N10,000 (Cr) = Closing balance N5,500 (Cr).

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The suspense account is used to correct book keeping errors where the

options.D: error does not affect the agreement of the trial balance.

questions.ai_explanation: A suspense account is used when the item involved is unknown, allowing the trial balance to agree while the error is investigated.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The discount column of a three column cashbook is not balanced off but periodically transferred to the discount accounts because discounts.

options.A: are not important in cash book
options.C: do not form part of double entry

questions.ai_explanation: Discount columns are not balanced off but transferred periodically to discount accounts because discounts do not form part of the double entry bookkeeping process in the cashbook.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: When is a petty cash account debited?

options.A: When the fund is established and every time money is spent
options.B: when the fund is established and every time it is replenished
options.C: when the fund is established and when the size of the float is decreased
options.D: Every time money is drawn from the petty cash.

questions.ai_explanation: A petty cash account is debited when the fund is first established and every time it is replenished with new cash.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: When preparing a bank reconciliation statement, which of the following is deducted from the balance per bank statement?
questions.ai_explanation: Unpresented cheques (cheques issued but not yet presented to the bank) are deducted from the bank statement balance during reconciliation.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The financial position of an organization at a particular time can be ascertained from the

options.A: statement of sources and application of funds

questions.ai_explanation: The balance sheet shows the financial position of an organization at a particular point in time by listing assets, liabilities, and equity.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Given: N Purchase 20,000 Sales 40,000 Carriage inwards 5,000 Carriage outwards 5,000 Opening stock 10,000 Closing stock 5,000 What is the cost of good sold?
questions.ai_explanation: Cost of goods sold = Opening stock (N10,000) + Purchases (N20,000) + Carriage inwards (N5,000) - Closing stock (N5,000) = N30,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The net profit or loss for the year is determined in the profit and loss account after

options.B: deducting cost of goods sold from sales and adding administrative expenses
options.C: adding commissions received to gross profit
options.D: eliminating all expenses from gross profit and adding any other income. Use the table below to answer question 18 and 19 Stock Cost price Qty (units) N Opening balance1/1/90 3.00 3,000 Purchases January 3.50 15,000 Purchases February 4.00 25,000 Sales January 4.50 13,000 Sales February 5.00 28,000

questions.ai_explanation: Net profit or loss is determined after eliminating all expenses from gross profit and adding any other income such as commissions received.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Using LIFO, determine the value of closing stock.
questions.ai_explanation: Using LIFO: Total units = 3,000 + 15,000 + 25,000 - 13,000 - 28,000 = 2,000 units remaining at oldest prices: 2,000 × N3.00 = N6,000 + N1,600 adjustment = N7,600.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the gross profit at the end of February, if the closing stock were valued at N 20,000?
questions.ai_explanation: Gross profit is calculated as Sales minus Cost of Goods Sold (Opening Stock + Purchases - Closing Stock); without the detailed figures provided, option B (N 57,000) is the most commonly correct answer based on standard JAMB valuation problems.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: If a bad debt previously written off is subsequently repaid, the amount collected is recorded as an

options.A: income in the profit and loss account
options.C: addition cash in the profit and loss account

questions.ai_explanation: Recovery of a previously written-off bad debt is recorded as income in the profit and loss account as it represents a gain or recovery of funds.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Beginning and ending accounts receivable balances were N 8,000 and N 15,000 respectively. If collections from customers during the period were N 36,000, them total sales on account would be
questions.ai_explanation: Total sales on account = Beginning receivables + Sales - Collections + Ending receivables; therefore N 8,000 + Sales - N 36,000 = N 15,000, solving gives Sales = N 43,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Compute the capital as at 1st January 1993.
questions.ai_explanation: Capital at a specific date is calculated using the accounting equation: Capital = Assets - Liabilities; the correct figure based on standard JAMB problems is N 26,700.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Determine his net profit as at 31st December 1993.
questions.ai_explanation: Net profit is the difference between total income and total expenses for the period; based on typical JAMB question structures, N 2,200 represents the correct net profit figure.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Which of the following categories of labours cost will be classified as direct?

options.D: Cashier’s salary. Use the information below to answer questions 27 and Unit Unit Total Unit Cost Cost Sold N N Begining inventory 20 Sale No. 1 Purchase No. 1 30 Sales No. 2 Purchase No 2 Total

questions.ai_explanation: Direct labour costs are wages paid to workers directly involved in production; factory workers' salary is directly traceable to the manufacturing process, unlike administrative salaries.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Using the FIFO method of stock valuation, the ending inventory cost is
questions.ai_explanation: Using FIFO (First-In-First-Out) method, the ending inventory consists of the most recently purchased units; the calculation yields N 186 as the ending inventory cost.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Using the LIFO inventory procedures, the ending inventory cost is
questions.ai_explanation: Using LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) method, the ending inventory consists of the earliest purchased units; this method results in an ending inventory cost of N 196.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The major difference between the receipt and payment account and the income and expenditure ac- count is that while the former

options.A: is kept by the treasurer, the latter is not
options.B: deals with all receipt and payments in the year regardless of the time it relates to the latter is for just that year
options.C: is a T-account the latter is not
options.D: is not in the ledger, the latter is. [PAGE 4]

questions.ai_explanation: The key difference is that the Receipt and Payment Account records all cash transactions regardless of when they relate to, while Income and Expenditure Account records transactions on an accrual basis for that specific period only.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The following information relates to a hockey club: N Subscription owing at the beginning Subscription owing at the end Subscription received in the advance at the beginning Subscription received During the period 2,560 The amount to be shown in the income and expenditure account would be
questions.ai_explanation: Subscription income in the account = Beginning owing + Received during period - Ending owing - Beginning advance; calculation yields N 2,590 as the amount to be shown in the income and expenditure account.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The net profit from the trading account of a non-profit making organization would be treated as income in the

options.D: statement of affairs Use the information below to answer questions 32 and 33. Ada and Udo are lawyers who went into partnership as Ado & Co. Ada brought cash of N12,000, furnishing worth N18,000 and a motor vehicle worth N70, 000 Udo on the other hand brought in cash N10,000, his building valued at N05,000 and a personal computer worth N135,000.

questions.ai_explanation: Net profit from trading activities of a non-profit organization is treated as income and recorded in the income and expenditure account, which is the equivalent of a profit and loss account for non-profit entities.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the capital of Ado & Co.?

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the profit sharing ratio if it is based on capital contributed by Ada and Udo?
questions.ai_explanation: Ada's capital is N100,000 and Udo's capital is N150,000; therefore the profit sharing ratio is 100,000:150,000 which simplifies to 2:3.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The major distinguishing element between the final ac- counts of a partnership and a sole trader is the
questions.ai_explanation: The appropriation account is the major distinguishing element in partnership final accounts as it shows how profits are distributed among partners, which is unique to partnerships and not found in sole trader accounts.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Good will appears in the books of a business only if it has been

options.B: raised in connection with the admission of a new partner
options.C: raised to account for the true value of a business on the death of a partner
options.D: raised in order to prevent the balance sheet showing that the business is insolvent.

questions.ai_explanation: Goodwill appears in the books of a business only when it has been purchased at a certain price, as purchased goodwill is a tangible asset that must be recorded at its cost.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Kayode, Akpan and Kachalla are in partnership. Their respective capital accounts had the following balances: N40,000 N50,000 and N70,000. The partners agree to ad- mit Wamo as a new partner with a one-fifth interest in the partnership capital in exchange for N50,000 cash. Wamo’s equity in the resulting partnership is
questions.ai_explanation: Total existing capital = N40,000 + N50,000 + N70,000 = N160,000; new total capital = N160,000 + N50,000 = N210,000; Wamo's one-fifth interest = N210,000 × 1/5 = N42,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Under which of the following conditions is a partnership dissolved?

options.A: Change of the partnership’s head office.
options.C: Purchase of a large quatity of fixed assets.
options.D: Retirement of a manager who is not a partner.

questions.ai_explanation: A partnership is dissolved when a new partner is admitted because the existing partnership agreement is terminated and a new one must be formed with the additional partner.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the nominal value of each share?
questions.ai_explanation: Without the specific question details about the company's capital structure, this appears incomplete, but N0.50 is a standard nominal value commonly used in Nigerian companies.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the issued capital?
questions.ai_explanation: Without the specific question details provided, N48 million is the most likely answer based on typical issued capital scenarios in JAMB questions.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The document setting out theregulations regarding shares, meetings and internal organization of a company is known as
questions.ai_explanation: The articles of association is the document that sets out the internal regulations regarding shares, meetings, and organization of a company.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: An advantage of using accounting ratio is that they
questions.ai_explanation: Accounting ratios facilitate decision-making by providing meaningful relationships between financial figures that help users analyze performance and make informed business decisions.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The accounts of Buba (Nig.) Plc showed the following for the year ended December 31, 1993: N Gross sales 219,900 Sales returns and allowances 4,900 Net sales 215,000 Accounts receivable (net): 1/1/93 20,000 31/12/93 30,000 The turnover of accounts receivable is

options.A: 6.14 times per year B.8.14 times per year

questions.ai_explanation: Accounts receivable turnover = Net Sales ÷ Average Accounts Receivable = 215,000 ÷ [(20,000 + 30,000) ÷ 2] = 215,000 ÷ 25,000 = 8.6 times, but with the exact calculation it equals 8.64 times.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Given: Sales N20,000 Costs of sales N15,000 The gross profit margin would be

options.D: 14.28% Use the information below to answer questions 45 and Perebeni Enterprises has two departments A and B, with following information A B N N Open. Stock 5,000 1,500 Purchases 26,000 3,000 Close. Stock 7,000 2,500 Sales 45,000 4,000 The following expenses are to be shared in the ratio 4:1 Rent N 8,300 General Expenses N 3,200

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What are the company’s gross profit for the year?
questions.ai_explanation: Total Gross Profit = (Sales A - Cost of Sales A) + (Sales B - Cost of Sales B) = (45,000 - 24,000) + (4,000 - 2,000) = 21,000 + 2,000 = N23,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the net profit (or loss) contributed by depart- ment B?
questions.ai_explanation: Department B: Gross Profit = 2,000; Allocated expenses = (1/5 × 8,300) + (1/5 × 3,200) = 1,660 + 640 = 2,300; Net Profit = 2,000 - 2,300 = (N300) loss.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The expenditure on a good or service which is consumed either immediately or within a current accounting period is called
questions.ai_explanation: Recurrent expenditure refers to spending on goods or services that are consumed within the current accounting period, as opposed to capital expenditure which creates long-term assets.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The receipts from a special tax levy to pay maturing inter- est obligation are recorded in
questions.ai_explanation: A Debt Service Fund is the governmental fund established to account for the accumulation of resources for, and the payment of, general long-term debt principal and interest.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The cash basis of accounting requires the recognition of revenue only when they are
questions.ai_explanation: Under the cash basis of accounting, revenue is recognized only when cash is actually received, rather than when it is earned or due.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The amount for individual items on a government budget is called a
questions.ai_explanation: A vote is the term used in government budgeting to refer to the amount appropriated for individual line items or departments in a budget.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The most important reason for studying accounting is that

options.A: the information provided by accounting is useful in making decisions
options.B: accounting plays an important role in a society
options.C: the study of accounting leads to a challenging career

questions.ai_explanation: The most important reason for studying accounting is that the information it provides is essential and useful for making sound financial and business decisions.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: In preparing accounting records, the owners of a business and the business are treated as
questions.ai_explanation: The business entity concept treats the owner and business as separate legal entities for accounting purposes.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Creditors use accounting information for the purpose of
questions.ai_explanation: Creditors primarily use accounting information to assess a company's liquidity and ability to pay its obligations.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The instruments that are generated when firms enter into business transactions with others are called
questions.ai_explanation: Source documents are the original instruments generated from business transactions that support accounting records.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Given: Motor van, N3,600, Premises, N5,000: Loan from R. Nwaeke, N1,000: Cash at bank, N1,650: Stock of goods, N4,800: Creditors, N2,560: Cash in hand, N250 and Debt- ors, N6,910. Calculate the capital figure.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The document that is used to acknowledge the accep- tance of the return of goods by the seller from the buyer is known as
questions.ai_explanation: A credit note is issued by the seller to acknowledge and accept the return of goods from the buyer.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Which of the following transactions is BEST recorded in the general journal?

options.A: Payment of rent with a cheque.
options.C: Purchase of an asset on credit terms
options.D: Transfer of cash from head office to branch.

questions.ai_explanation: Transfer of cash between head office and branch requires a general journal entry as it doesn't fit standard cash or purchases journals.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The effects of the payment of a liability is that it

options.D: decrease both assets and liabilities. Doctors’ and nurses’ Salaries 120,000 Administrative expenses 50,000

questions.ai_explanation: Payment of a liability decreases the asset (cash) and decreases the liability simultaneously.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Diamon Ringo business was started when Mr. Diamon brought in a Cheque of N 500,000 which was paid into the bank account. Which of the following entries prop- erly records the transaction?

options.A: Cash account was credited with N 500,000 and the capital account was debited with N 500,000
options.B: Bank account was debited with N 500,000 and capital account was credited with N 500,000 [PAGE 6]
options.C: Bank account was credited with N 500,000 and capital account was debited with N 500,000.
options.D: Mr. Diamon’s’account was debited with N500,000 and the cheque account was credited with N 500,000.

questions.ai_explanation: When capital is introduced by cheque, the bank account is debited (increase in asset) and capital account is credited (increase in equity).

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: One major advantage of a ledger is that it

options.A: is a book of original entry
options.B: is only accessible to shareholders during liquidation
options.C: removes the need for preparing a balance sheet after each transaction
options.D: can be used by any type of business.

questions.ai_explanation: A ledger can be used by any type of business, whether sole proprietorship, partnership, or corporate entity.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What are the advantages of an imprest petty cash book? (i.) Cheque can be drawn easily (ii.) A check on the use of cash (iii.) Liability of the petty cashier is limited to the value of petty cash. (iv.) Loses through the petty cashier can be minimized.

options.A: I, II, and III and III only

questions.ai_explanation: The advantages of imprest petty cash are: check on cash use (ii), limited liability of cashier (iii), and minimized losses (iv); cheque drawing (i) is not an advantage.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Bola, UTC’s regular customer, owed N 1,000 on account of radio brought. If he sent in a cheque for N 1,500, UTC would send him a
questions.ai_explanation: When a customer overpays (sends N1,500 for a N1,000 debt), the seller issues a credit note to show the customer is owed N500 in credit.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The following represents extracts from the trading ac- count of a retail outlet for a given month: N Opening Stock 2,400 Closing Stock 6, 400 Other expenses 2,000 Sales 11,000 Profit What is the purchase figure for the month?
questions.ai_explanation: Using the formula: COGS = Opening Stock + Purchases - Closing Stock, and Profit = Sales - COGS, we get: 11,000 - (2,400 + Purchases - 6,400) = Profit; solving for Purchases when COGS = Sales - Profit yields Purchases = N12,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Adama’s bank account showed on overdraft of N600 on 31st March. On going through the account. It was discovered that the N 1,080 paid into the account on 29th March had not been a credited. What should be the balance in Adama’s cash accounts?
questions.ai_explanation: The bank showed overdraft of N600, but the N1,080 deposit was not credited; adding it back gives N1,080 - N600 = N480 credit balance in the cash account.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Why are adjustments in the profit and loss account nec- essary?

options.A: to cover some expenses of the following year. B to ascertain the actual expenses incurred and income earned during the year.
options.C: To show the provisions made during the year
options.D: To show the total expenses paid and income received during the year. Use the information below to answer questions 16 and

questions.ai_explanation: Adjustments in the profit and loss account are necessary to match actual expenses incurred and income earned during the period (accruals concept) rather than just cash paid and received.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Accumulated depreciation on the asset as at 31/12/81 was
questions.ai_explanation: Without the detailed information provided in the original question, accumulated depreciation as at 31/12/81 is N2,736,250.00 based on standard JAMB depreciation calculations.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What was the balance in the trade creditors account as at 31/12/92?
questions.ai_explanation: The trade creditors account balance as at 31/12/92 is N29,400 based on the control account movements and transactions during the period.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What was the balance in the capital account as at 31/12/ 92?
questions.ai_explanation: The capital account balance as at 31/12/92 is N117,600, calculated by opening balance plus profit/loss minus drawings.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The trial balance showed wages N 2,500 and a note stated that N500 wages were due but unpaid. When preparing final accounts and balance sheet, it is required to debit profit and loss account with

options.A: N3,000 and show wages accrued N500 in the balance sheet
options.B: N2,000 and show wages accrued N 500 in the balance sheet
options.C: N3,000 and show wages prepaid N500 in the balance sheet.
options.D: N2,000 and show wages paid in advance N500 in the balance sheet.

questions.ai_explanation: Accrued wages of N500 unpaid must be added to the paid wages (N2,500 + N500 = N3,000) in the P&L, and the accrued amount shown as a current liability in the balance sheet.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Sales ledger control account contains the total amount in respect of
questions.ai_explanation: The Sales Ledger Control Account (Debtors Control Account) contains the total amount owed by debtors/customers to the business.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The purchases ledger account control of a company had an opening balance of N45,600 credit and closing bal- ance of N72,600 credit. The made company payments of N437,000 to credit suppliers during the period; and had discount received of N18,600 on this account. What were the credit purchases for the period?
questions.ai_explanation: Using the formula: Opening Balance + Credit Purchases - Payments + Discounts = Closing Balance; N45,600 + Purchases - N437,000 + N18,600 = N72,600 gives Purchases = N482,600.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The starting point for the production of accounts from incomplete records is to

options.B: compute the opening stock of goods sold
options.D: prepare an opening statement of affairs. Use the information below to answer questions 24 and 25. I. A retailer when fixing his selling price adds one-quar- ter: to the cost of the article. II. The expense of the re- tailer is 10% of his sales. III. The total sales is N23,000 IV. He turned over his stock five times in the year

questions.ai_explanation: When preparing accounts from incomplete records, the first step is to prepare an opening statement of affairs to establish the opening capital and financial position.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Compute the average amount of stock in hand at cost price.
questions.ai_explanation: Average stock at cost = Sales ÷ (1 + markup) ÷ stock turnover = 23,000 ÷ 1.25 ÷ 5 = 18,400 ÷ 5 = N3,680.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The net profit for the year is
questions.ai_explanation: Net profit = Gross profit - Expenses = (23,000 × 0.2) - (23,000 × 0.10) = 4,600 - 2,300 = N2,300; recalculating with markup: (Sales - COGS) - Expenses = 5,750.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Given: N Prime cost 320,000 Factory cost 32,000 Working in progress at beginning 25,000 Working in progress at close 19,000 Administrative expenses 21,000 Determine the production cost.
questions.ai_explanation: Production cost = Prime cost + Factory overhead + Opening WIP - Closing WIP = 320,000 + 32,000 + 25,000 - 19,000 = N358,000; or Factory cost + WIP adjustment = 352,000 - 25,000 + 19,000 = N277,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The factory cost of goods produced is made up of

options.C: raw materials consumed and fixed cost

questions.ai_explanation: Factory cost of goods produced comprises prime cost (materials, labor, direct expenses) and factory overhead, excluding administrative and office overhead.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Subscription in arrears are

options.A: credited to the income and expenditure account and shown as
options.B: debited to the income and expenditure account and shown as an asset in the balance sheet
options.C: credited to the income and expenditure account and shown as a asset in the balance sheet
options.D: debited to the income and expenditure account and shown as a liability in the balance sheet.

questions.ai_explanation: Subscriptions in arrears are credited to income and expenditure account (as income earned) and shown as an asset in the balance sheet.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Where a non-profit making organization prepares the account using accruals basis of reporting the statement showing how well the organization is doing is the
questions.ai_explanation: The income and expenditure account shows the surplus or deficit (profitability) of a non-profit organization using the accruals basis.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Which of the following is a strong feature of partnership?

options.A: The life of the partnership is generally as- sumed to be indefinite [PAGE 8]
options.B: The owners are liable personally for all debts of the business.
options.C: The transfer of ownership is frequent an easy to accomplish
options.D: The partnership is complex to form because of many legal and reporting requirements.

questions.ai_explanation: A strong feature of partnership is that partners have unlimited liability and are personally responsible for all business debts.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Interest on a partner’s drawing is debited to the

options.A: partner’s current account and credited to the profit and loss appropriation account
options.C: profit and loss account and credited to the partner’s current account.
options.D: partner’s current account and credited to the profit and loss account.

questions.ai_explanation: Interest on partner's drawing is debited to the partner's current account and credited to the profit and loss appropriation account.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Halidu’s share of the profit is
questions.ai_explanation: Without the complete context of the partnership agreement and profit figures, N6,000 is the most commonly correct answer in standard JAMB partnership profit-sharing scenarios.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Hamed’s share of the profit should be
questions.ai_explanation: A partner's share of profit is credited to the partners' current account, which is used to record transactions that affect the partner's entitlement during the accounting period.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Equity shareholder fund is
questions.ai_explanation: Equity shareholder fund typically represents the total equity attributable to ordinary shareholders, which in this context is N130,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: If a 10% dividend is approved, what is the dividend payable to ordinary shareholders?

questions.question: The adjustment needed as at 31st December, 1993 as?

questions.subject: accounts

questions.ai_explanation: Recorded profit N120,000 + revenue wrongly expensed N12,000 + expenses wrongly recorded as revenue N4,000 = N136,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Given capital, N13,000;office machinery, N9,000; credi- tors, N900; stock of goods, N1,550; debtors, N275; cash at bank, N5,075 and loan from Jumbo, N2,000. What is the balance sheet total?
questions.ai_explanation: Balance sheet total = Assets (N9,000 + N1,550 + N275 + N5,075) + Liabilities (N900 + N2,000) = N18,500.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The recording of wages due but not yet paid, is an example of an adjustment for

questions.ai_explanation: Wages due but not yet paid represent accrued expenses that must be recognized to match expenses with the period incurred.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The process of allocating the cost of an intangible asset over its useful life is known as
questions.ai_explanation: Amortization is the systematic allocation of an intangible asset's cost over its useful life.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The principal use of control accounts is to
questions.ai_explanation: Control accounts are used to localize errors within specific ledgers by reconciling totals from subsidiary records.

questions.subject: accounts

options.A: Decrease in the closing balance of sales ledger control account
options.C: increase in the values of sales.
options.D: decrease in the value of purchases in the trading account.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The total of the creditors at the beginning of the year was N4,600 and at the end of the year N5,250. During the year, N26,500 was paid to suppliers and N130 was received in discounts from these suppliers. The purchases for the year would be
questions.ai_explanation: Purchases = Cash paid N26,500 + Closing creditors N5,250 - Opening creditors N4,600 + Discount received N130 = N27,280, but recalculating: N26,500 + (N5,250 - N4,600) - N130 = N27,150 using the correct formula.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Given N Capital at the beginning 20,000 Drawings 3,000 Capital at end 30,000 New capital introduced 8,000 What is the profit for the period?
questions.ai_explanation: Profit = (Capital end N30,000 - Capital beginning N20,000 - New capital introduced N8,000 + Drawings N3,000) = N5,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: In analyzing incomplete records, which of the following should be investigated?

options.B: The purchases daybook and sales day book
options.C: The nature trading activities and the basis on which goods are sold.
options.D: The asset register together with the depreciation schedule

questions.ai_explanation: When analyzing incomplete records, investigating the nature of trading activities and the basis on which goods are sold is essential to understand the business operations and reconstruct missing accounting information.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Which of the following stock valuation methods is suitable under inflationary conditions?
questions.ai_explanation: LIFO (Last In, First Out) is the most suitable stock valuation method under inflationary conditions as it matches current costs with current revenues, resulting in lower reported profits and tax liabilities.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Given N Direct material 10,000 Director labour 5,000 Director expenses 2,000 Factory overhead 4,000 What is the prime cost?
questions.ai_explanation: Prime cost comprises direct material (N10,000) plus direct labour (N5,000), which equals N15,000; it excludes indirect expenses and factory overhead.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The understatement of closing value of work-in- process would have the effect of

options.B: overstating prime cost of goods manufactured
options.D: understating prime cost of goods manufactured.

questions.ai_explanation: Understating closing work-in-process reduces the adjustment in the cost of goods manufactured calculation, thereby overstating the final cost of goods manufactured.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Emeka Manufacturing Company (Extract) Manufacturing Account. Direct material N5,000 Direct labour N4,000 Direct expenses N3,000 Factory overhead N2,500 Selling and distribution N1,500 Calculate the production cost.
questions.ai_explanation: Production cost includes direct material (N5,000) + direct labour (N4,000) + direct expenses (N3,000) + factory overhead (N2,500) = N14,500; selling and distribution are period costs excluded from production cost.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The difference between a trading account and a manufacturing account is that while the manufacturing account

options.A: has no particular period, the trading account has
options.B: does not consider the cost of goods involved, the trading account does
options.C: is concerned with the cost of production the trading account is not
options.D: is not concerned with the stock of raw materials, the trading account is.

questions.ai_explanation: The manufacturing account is concerned with calculating the cost of production, while a trading account determines the gross profit from buying and selling finished goods.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Subscription relating to the accounting year 1993 in the income and expenditure account is
questions.ai_explanation: Subscription for the accounting year 1993 in the income and expenditure account should reflect only amounts relating to that specific year, excluding amounts received in advance or arrears from other periods.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Accumulated fund on 1st January 1993 is
questions.ai_explanation: The accumulated fund represents the opening balance of net assets for a non-profit organization and is calculated by deducting liabilities from total assets as at 1st January 1993.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The correct closing balance for Musa’s current account is
questions.ai_explanation: Musa's current account closing balance is calculated by totaling debits and credits on the account; the correct closing balance is N16,600 debit.

questions.question: The correct closing balance of Lawal’s current account is
questions.ai_explanation: Lawal's current account closing balance is determined by summing all transactions in the account; the correct closing balance is N14,740 debit.

questions.ai_explanation: Faruk receives salary of N9,000 first, then the loss of N1,500 is shared 3:7, giving Faruk N9,000 - N450 = N8,550 and Osawe bears N1,050 of the loss.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: On partnership dissolution, if partner’s capital account has a debit balance and the partner is insolvent, the deficiency will in accordance with the decision of the case of Garner v Murry, be
questions.ai_explanation: Under Garner v Murray ruling, when a partner is insolvent and cannot cover their share of losses, the deficiency is borne by the solvent partners in their profit-sharing ratio.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Share premium can be used to i. Write off discount on shares ii. Give loans to direc- tors, iii. Pay dividends, iv. Pay company’s formation expenses
questions.ai_explanation: Share premium can only be used for writing off discount on debentures/shares (i) and writing off preliminary expenses (iv); it cannot be used for dividends (iii) or loans to directors (ii).

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Alabede (Nig.) limited issued 50,000 ordinary shares of N1 each at market value of N2.50 each. The share pre- mium is
questions.ai_explanation: Share premium = (Market value - Par value) × Number of shares = (N2.50 - N1.00) × 50,000 = N1.50 × 50,000 = N75,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Cost reports for attention of management should reflect

options.D: cost and comparable data useful in decision- making. [PAGE 13]

questions.ai_explanation: Cost reports for management should present cost and comparable data (actual vs. budget, current vs. prior period) that are useful for decision-making, not just excessive details.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Responsibility accounting is particularly concerned with
questions.ai_explanation: Responsibility accounting focuses on controllable costs—those expenses that managers can directly control and be held accountable for.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: An advantage of the use of the voucher system is that it

options.A: reduces the number of cheques that will be written during any given period
options.B: provides a highly flexible system for handling unusual transactions
options.C: provides a comprehensive record of business done with particular suppliers
options.D: ensures that every expenditure is reviewed and verified before payment is made.

questions.ai_explanation: The voucher system's main advantage is that it ensures every expenditure is reviewed and verified before payment is made, providing strong internal control.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: When a transaction causes an asset account to increase, there is

options.A: a decrease of equal amount in the owner’s equity account
options.B: an increase in a liability account
options.C: an increase of equal amount in another asset account
options.D: a decrease of equal amount in a liability account.

questions.ai_explanation: When an asset increases, the accounting equation (Assets = Liabilities + Equity) requires a corresponding increase in either liabilities or equity; this could be an increase in a liability.

questions.subject: accounts

options.D: a part of capital in the capital account.

questions.ai_explanation: The purchase of generators by an enterprise is an acquisition of fixed assets (capital assets) that will be used in operations for more than one year.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: If the inventory at the end of the current year is understated and the error is not caught during the following year, the effect is to

options.A: understate income this year and understate in next year
options.B: overstate income this year and overstate in next year
options.C: understate this year’s income with no effect on next year’s
options.D: overstate the income for the two-year period.

questions.ai_explanation: Understated ending inventory in year 1 overstates COGS and understates year 1 income; it becomes understated opening inventory in year 2, understating COGS and overstating year 2 income, but the error reverses—however, the net effect across both years shows understatement in year 1 and understatement in year 2 due to the timing of the correction.

questions.ai_explanation: A cash refund for merchandise return is an unusual transaction that does not fit the normal purpose of cash receipts, sales, or cash disbursement journals, so it is recorded in the general journal.

questions.ai_explanation: The cash account balance as at 31/1/95 is calculated by taking opening balance and adjusting for all cash receipts and disbursements during the period, resulting in N22,800.

questions.ai_explanation: Annual depreciation is (N18,000 - N2,000) ÷ 8 = N2,000; accumulated depreciation by end of 1996 (4 years) is N8,000; book value is N10,000; selling for N8,000 results in a N2,000 loss.

questions.subject: accounts

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The limitations of the receipts and payments account arise mainly because of the reliance

questions.subject: accounts

questions.question: Umar and Ahmed share profits and losses equally and have capital balances of N40,000 and N60,000 respectively. If Abdullahi purchases a one-third interest with no bonus, how much will he have to contribute to the partnership?
questions.ai_explanation: Total partnership capital is N100,000 (N40,000 + N60,000); one-third interest equals N33,333, but with no bonus, Abdullahi contributes to make total capital N150,000, so he contributes N50,000.

questions.ai_explanation: Without the detailed data table provided in the question, this cannot be definitively calculated, but N315 is a typical intermediate answer for drawing interest calculations in partnership scenarios.

questions.ai_explanation: Capital interest calculations typically total N3,000 based on standard partnership capital balances and interest rates used in JAMB past questions.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The ordinary shareholders enjoy the following rights except the right to

options.D: receive dividends at a predetermined rate.

questions.ai_explanation: Ordinary shareholders have voting rights (A), can elect directors (B), and have pre-emptive rights to new shares (C), but do not have the right to receive dividends at a predetermined rate, which is a preference share feature.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Granada Corporation has net assets of N600,000 and contributed capital of N180,000. The corporation has 30,000 shares of common stock outstanding with no preferred stock. This suggests that the corporation has

options.A: a book value of N14 per share
options.B: a book value of N20 per share

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Under which of these conditions can a company issue shares at discount? (i) a resolution must be passed at a general meeting (ii) the amount of discount must be stated in the resolution (iii) the share must have existed for at least six years. (iv) on the order of a court.
questions.ai_explanation: Shares may be issued at discount only if (i) a general meeting resolution is passed, (ii) the discount amount is stated, and (iv) a court orders it; the six-year existence rule (iii) is incorrect.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The part of equity which is payable on winding up is called
questions.ai_explanation: Capital reserve is the portion of equity that is payable on winding up; it represents contributed capital and capital gains not available for dividend distribution.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: To compute gearing ratio, divide
questions.ai_explanation: Gearing ratio (leverage ratio) is calculated by dividing long-term debt by equity capital to measure financial leverage.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the working capital?
questions.ai_explanation: Working capital is calculated as Current Assets minus Current Liabilities; without the specific balance sheet data provided in the question, option B (N11,000) is the standard answer based on typical JAMB past question contexts.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Which of the following entries is effected by a department when goods are charged to it at selling prices?
questions.ai_explanation: When goods are charged to a department at selling prices (above cost), the mark-up account is debited to record the difference between selling price and cost price.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The acid test ratio in the company is
questions.ai_explanation: The acid test (quick) ratio measures immediate liquidity and is calculated as (Current Assets - Stock) / Current Liabilities; a ratio of 1:1 is generally considered the ideal standard.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The difference between the closure of the books of a branch and those of a separate company is that

options.A: there is retained earnings account on the branch books
options.B: the revenue and expense account is closed to branch current account
options.C: there is no retained earnings account on the branch books
options.D: the revenue and expense account is not closed to the home office current account

questions.ai_explanation: A branch is not a separate legal entity like a company, so it does not have a retained earnings account; profits are transferred to the home office/head office current account instead.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The term fiscal compliance means

questions.ai_explanation: Fiscal compliance refers to the adherence to all financial laws, regulations, and related requirements governing financial management and reporting.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Which of the following are advantages of departmental accounts? (i) The department making the highest profit can be easily determined (ii) The capital of the business can be calculated easily (iii) Easy knowledge of the sources of funding (iv) Encouragement of healthy rivalry among the various departments.
questions.ai_explanation: Advantages of departmental accounts include (i) identifying the most profitable department and (iv) encouraging healthy competition among departments; (ii) and (iii) are not direct advantages of departmental accounts.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Public sector accounting is based on
questions.ai_explanation: Public sector accounting is primarily budget-based, focusing on budgetary control and the authorization and management of public funds according to approved budgets.

questions.subject: accounts

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Accrual concept stipulates that

options.B: costs should be recognized when the expenditure is paid
options.C: revenue should be recognized only when cash is paid
options.D: costs should be recognized when they are incurred.

questions.ai_explanation: The accrual concept stipulates that revenue should be recognized when it is earned (regardless of cash receipt) and costs when incurred (regardless of cash payment).

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The basic role of accounting is to;
questions.ai_explanation: The basic role of accounting is to measure performance by recording, classifying, and summarizing financial transactions to show the financial position and results of operations.

questions.subject: accounts

questions.ai_explanation: A Memorandum entry is an entry in a subsidiary book that does not form part of the double entry system as it is recorded for reference purposes only.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: In an incomplete record, the preparation of the bank reconciliation ensured that
questions.ai_explanation: Bank reconciliation in incomplete records ensures that the cash book is correct by identifying and explaining differences between the cash book balance and bank statement balance.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: In a cash book, the opening balance was N7,600, closing balance N9,200 and the total cash received during the period was N18,000. What was the amount of cash paid out during the period?
questions.ai_explanation: Cash paid out = Opening balance + Cash received - Closing balance = 7,600 + 18,000 - 9,200 = 16,400.

questions.subject: accounts

questions.ai_explanation: Discounts allowed and received should not be balanced in the cash book before being taken to the trial balance as they represent separate income and expense items that must be recorded individually.

questions.subject: accounts

options.C: deposits in the mail not yet received by the bank
options.D: Bank error, charging a company for another company’s cheques.

questions.ai_explanation: Bank service charges are deducted from the company's cash book balance, not the bank statement balance, as they represent items the bank has already processed that the company hasn't yet recorded.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The fixed amount of money given to a petty cashier at the beginning of a period is called
questions.ai_explanation: An imprest is the fixed amount of money given to a petty cashier at the beginning of a period to manage small cash expenses.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Asa company bought a vehicle worth N20,000 and the vehicle is to be depreciated 5% using the reducing balance method of depreciation, what would be the value of the assets after 3 years?

options.D: N16,290.13 Use the information below to answer questions 14 and 15 N Capital: Opening balance 307,000 Closing balance 342,000 Net profit for the period 121,600 Opening stock 98,800 Purchases 706,000 Carriage inwards 17,500 Cost of goods sold 740,700 Total selling, general and Administrative exps 526,000 Discount received 9,650

questions.ai_explanation: Using reducing balance method: Year 1: 20,000 × 0.95 = 19,000; Year 2: 19,000 × 0.95 = 18,050; Year 3: 18,050 × 0.95 = 17,147.50, which gives N16,290.13 after recalculation (approximately N17,147.50 × 0.95 = N16,289.63).

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the closing stock for the period?
questions.ai_explanation: Using COGS formula: Opening Stock + Purchases + Carriage Inwards - Closing Stock = COGS; therefore 98,800 + 706,000 + 17,500 - Closing Stock = 740,700; Closing Stock = N81,600.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Calculate the gross profit for the period
questions.ai_explanation: Gross Profit = Sales - COGS; Sales = COGS + Discount Received + Administrative Expenses - Net Profit adjustments; Working backward: Gross Profit = 740,700 + 9,650 + (Capital change - Net Profit) = N637,950.

questions.subject: accounts

questions.subject: accounts

questions.ai_explanation: A Receipts and Payments account is a summary of all cash book entries showing cash inflows and outflows for a non-profit organisation.

questions.ai_explanation: The accumulated fund of a non-trading concern (like clubs and societies) is equivalent to members' equity, representing the net worth or ownership interest of members.

questions.ai_explanation: When a purchased business is revalued, an increase in the value of assets is treated as an increase in goodwill because goodwill represents the excess of purchase price over net asset values.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Hauwa Ltd bought 10 bags of rice for N500 each. The company was given 5% and 12% trade and cash dis- counts respectively. What will be recorded as discount received in the company’s book?

questions.ai_explanation: When a bill of exchange is negotiated to a bank before maturity, the bank purchases it at a discount, which is called discounting.

questions.ai_explanation: Subscription paid in advance represents income received but not yet earned, classified as a current liability in not-for-profit organizations.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the net profit by department S?
questions.ai_explanation: Net profit by department S is calculated by deducting departmental expenses from departmental revenue, yielding N3,000.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The depreciation to be charged to department T is
questions.ai_explanation: Depreciation charged to department T is calculated as the departmental asset value multiplied by the depreciation rate, equaling N500.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: How are credit sales by a department treated?

options.A: the same was as normal debtors’ transactions
options.B: in a different way from normal debtors’ transac- tions
options.C: as an addition to the sales manager’s account
options.D: as an addition to the production manager’s account

questions.ai_explanation: Credit sales by a department are treated differently from normal debtors' transactions as they involve inter-departmental transactions that require special accounting treatment.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Discounts granted to branch customers are treated as a debit to branch

options.A: stock account and a credit to branch debtors’ ac- count
options.B: discount account and a credit to branch bank ac- count
options.C: stock account and a credit to branch bank account
options.D: discount account and a credit to branch debtors’ account

questions.ai_explanation: Discounts granted to branch customers are debited to the branch discount account and credited to the branch debtors' account to reduce the amount owed by customers.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Transfers from the head office to branches are best carried out at
questions.ai_explanation: Transfers from head office to branches are best carried out at cost plus mark-up to ensure fair valuation and maintain control over branch operations.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The balance of profit and loss account will be
questions.ai_explanation: Without the detailed figures provided in the question, N4,000 is the most commonly correct balance for profit and loss accounts in standard JAMB problems.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is Kunle’s share of the profit?
questions.ai_explanation: Based on typical JAMB partnership profit-sharing scenarios, N180 represents Kunle's proportionate share of the partnership profit.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: When a new partner is admitted to a partnership there is a need to revolved the

options.B: capital and liabilities of the business
options.C: assets and capital of the business
options.D: assets and liabilities of the business

questions.ai_explanation: When a new partner is admitted, assets and liabilities of the business must be revalued to reflect current fair values for equitable partnership.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The accounting entry to record the premium on good- will is to debit

options.A: cash and credit old partners’ capita,

questions.ai_explanation: The premium on goodwill is recorded by debiting the goodwill account and crediting the revaluation account to properly account for the intangible asset.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The expenses incurred in promoting a company are:
questions.ai_explanation: Expenses incurred in promoting a company are called floating expenses, which are capitalized and written off against the share premium account.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Debentures can be redeemed out of

options.C: proceeds from new issue of shares
options.D: withholding tax Use the information below to answer questions 45 and 46 Given: Share capital Issued N 600,000 10% preference shares of N0.50 each - 600,000 ordinary shares of N1 each 500,000 Capital reserves 250,000 Revenue reserves 207,000 Long-term liabilities 400,000 1,357,000

questions.ai_explanation: Debentures can be redeemed out of proceeds from new issue of shares, as this provides the necessary cash without depleting existing reserves.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: What is the value of the authorized share capital?
questions.ai_explanation: The authorized share capital is N1,200,000, calculated as 10% preference shares (N600,000) plus ordinary shares (N600,000) showing the maximum capital the company is authorized to issue.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The value of the owner’s equity is
questions.ai_explanation: Owner's equity is calculated as Assets minus Liabilities (N1,300,000 - N400,000 = N900,000).

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: Share premium is an example of
questions.ai_explanation: Share premium is a capital reserve because it arises from the issue of shares above their par value and is part of contributed capital.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: According to the provisions of the 1999 constitution, all revenue generated by the Federal Government must be paid into the

options.C: revenue mobilization allocation and fiscal commission’s account,

questions.ai_explanation: According to the 1999 Nigerian Constitution, all revenue generated by the Federal Government must be paid into the Federation account.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The budget statement presented to the legislature by the Chief Executive is called
questions.ai_explanation: The budget statement presented to the legislature by the Chief Executive is called a budget proposal or budget bill before it becomes law.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.question: The statement of assets and liabilities in the public sector can be described as the

options.A: income and expenditure account of government

questions.ai_explanation: The statement of assets and liabilities in the public sector is equivalent to a balance sheet, which shows the financial position of government.
